headline: Malaysia Hosts 2.5 Million Documented Migrant Workers, Persistent Tier 2 Trafficking Concerns
context: MOF data placed registered migrant workers in Malaysia at ~2.5 million in August 2024. The US 2025 TIP Report kept Malaysia at Tier 2, citing weak prosecution of forced labour in palm oil and disposable-glove manufacturing.

cards.big: Malaysia hosts ~2.5 million documented migrant workers. The US TIP Report keeps flagging the same forced-labour patterns.
cards.sub: Malaysia retained Tier 2 in the 2025 TIP Report, with the State Department citing weak prosecution in palm oil and disposable-glove manufacturing.

cards.big: Employment Act 1955 amendments took effect 1 January 2023, extending core protections to all private-sector employees including foreign workers.
cards.sub: Indicators like passport confiscation, wage theft and debt bondage appear across sectors. The 2025 TIP says credible allegations went unaddressed.

cards.big: The US TIP Report's Tier 2 status reflects increasing efforts but unmet minimum standards in several key areas.
cards.sub: The 2025 report noted fewer trafficker convictions and no labour-trafficking prosecutions in palm oil or glove manufacturing despite ongoing reports.

cards.big: Foreign workers are officially allowed in manufacturing, construction, agriculture, plantations and services.
cards.sub: MOF end-August 2024: Bangladesh 37.8%, Indonesia 23.7%, Nepal 16.7% are the top source countries. No single official aggregate of their economic contribution was located.

cards.big: Tier 2 is not a passing grade. Malaysia was Tier 3 in 2014; the trajectory has not reached structural compliance with TVPA minimum standards.
Like a factory that treats its workers' welfare as a compliance line item: essential to hire, not to protect.
cards.sub: Documentation buys access to labour but not enforcement of the protections already in law.

cards.big: The 2023 Employment Act expansion is necessary but not sufficient. The 2025 TIP urges investigating and prosecuting credible allegations, especially in palm oil and gloves.
